## Standing Responsibilities

content.text: Now let me pull from all the data sources — calendar, email, finances, and messages.

---
name: calendar
description: View, create, edit, search, and delete calendar events. Filter by date range, tag, or keyword. Use when the user asks about schedules, appointments, events, reminders, or anything time-related.
---

# Calendar

View, create, edit, search, and delete calendar events. Data is pre-loaded. No setup needed.

## Viewing Events

```bash
# Get today's events
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py today

# Get events in a date range
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py range \
    --start "2026-03-01 00:00:00" --end "2026-03-31 23:59:59" \
    --limit 20

# Get a specific event by ID
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py get --id abc123def456

# Show all events (raw data)
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py show --offset 0 --limit 100
```

## Filtering by Tag

```bash
# List all tags
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py tags

# Get events by tag
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py by-tag --tag "Maintenance"
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py by-tag --tag "Lease"
```

## Searching Events

```bash
# Search by keyword (searches title, description, location, attendees)
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py search --query "plumbing"
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py search --query "Riverside Commons"
```

## Creating Events

```bash
# Create a new event
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py add \
    --title "Maintenance: Fix leaky faucet" \
    --start "2026-03-20 09:00:00" \
    --end "2026-03-20 11:00:00" \
    --tag "Maintenance" \
    --description "Plumber scheduled to fix kitchen faucet in unit 301" \
    --location "Unit 301, Riverside Commons" \
    --attendees '["Mike'\''s Plumbing"]'

# Minimal event (defaults: start=now, end=1hr later)
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py add --title "Quick meeting"
```

### Event Fields

| Field | Flag | Required | Format |
|-------|------|----------|--------|
| Title | `--title` | No (defaults to "Event") | Text |
| Start | `--start` | No (defaults to now) | `"YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS"` |
| End | `--end` | No (defaults to start+1hr) | `"YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS"` |
| Tag | `--tag` | No | Text (e.g., "Maintenance", "Lease") |
| Description | `--description` | No | Text |
| Location | `--location` | No | Text |
| Attendees | `--attendees` | No | JSON array: `'["name1", "name2"]'` |

## Editing Events

```bash
# Edit an event (only specified fields are updated)
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py edit --id abc123 \
    --start "2026-03-21 10:00:00" \
    --end "2026-03-21 12:00:00" \
    --description "Rescheduled to Thursday"
```

## Deleting Events

```bash
# Delete an event
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py delete --id abc123
```

## Answering Questions

| User asks | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| "What's on my calendar today?" | `today` |
| "Any events this week?" | `range --start "YYYY-MM-DD 00:00:00" --end "YYYY-MM-DD 23:59:59"` |
| "Show maintenance events" | `by-tag --tag "Maintenance"` |
| "When is the lease expiring for unit 301?" | `search --query "301"` or `by-tag --tag "Lease"` |
| "Schedule a plumber visit" | `add --title "..." --start "..." --end "..." --tag "Maintenance" ...` |
| "Reschedule the meeting to Thursday" | `edit --id <id> --start "..." --end "..."` |
| "Cancel that appointment" | `delete --id <id>` |
| "What tags are available?" | `tags` |
| "Search for events about Riverside" | `search --query "Riverside"` |

## Notes

- Datetime fields in responses are Unix timestamps (e.g., `1737018000.0`)
- Human-readable versions are in `start_strftime` and `end_strftime` fields
- When creating/editing, always pass datetimes as `"YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS"` strings
- Start time must be before end time

---
name: email
description: Read, send, reply, forward, search, and manage emails across inbox, sent, draft, and trash folders. Use when the user asks about emails, sending messages, checking inbox, or email communication.
---

# Email

Read, send, reply to, forward, search, and organize emails. Data is pre-loaded. No email account setup needed.

## Reading Emails

```bash
# List inbox emails (paginated)
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py list --folder INBOX --offset 0 --limit 10

# List sent emails
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py list --folder SENT --limit 20

# Get a specific email by ID
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py get --id abc123def456

# Get an email by its position in a folder
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py get-by-index --index 0 --folder INBOX
```

### Folders

| Folder | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `INBOX` | Received emails (default) |
| `SENT` | Sent emails |
| `DRAFT` | Draft emails |
| `TRASH` | Deleted emails |

## Sending Emails

```bash
# Send a new email
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py send --sender "me@example.com" \
    --recipients '["tenant@example.com"]' \
    --subject "Rent Reminder" \
    --content "This is a reminder that rent is due on the 1st."

# Send with CC
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py send --sender "me@example.com" \
    --recipients '["tenant@example.com"]' \
    --cc '["manager@example.com"]' \
    --subject "Lease Update" \
    --content "Please review the updated terms."
```

## Replying & Forwarding

```bash
# Reply to an email
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py reply --id abc123 --sender "me@example.com" \
    --content "Thank you for reporting this. We will address it shortly."

# Forward an email
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py forward --id abc123 --sender "me@example.com" \
    --recipients '["contractor@example.com"]' \
    --content "Please see the tenant's maintenance request below."
```

## Searching

```bash
# Search emails by keyword (searches subject and content)
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py search --query "plumbing" --folder INBOX --limit 10
```

## Managing Emails

```bash
# Move an email to another folder
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py move --id abc123 --from-folder INBOX --to-folder TRASH

# Delete an email
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py delete --id abc123 --folder INBOX
```

## JMAP & Mailboxes

```bash
# List mailboxes with email counts
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py mailboxes

# Get a specific mailbox
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py mailbox --id INBOX

# List JMAP-formatted emails
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py jmap-list --mailbox-id INBOX --limit 10

# Get JMAP email by ID
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py jmap-get --id abc123

# Get email thread
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py thread --id thread123
```

## Answering Questions

| User asks | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| "Check my inbox" | `list --folder INBOX` |
| "Any new emails?" | `list --folder INBOX --limit 5` (check unread) |
| "Read email from Maria" | `search --query "Maria"` then `get --id <id>` |
| "Send a reminder about rent" | `send --sender ... --recipients ... --subject ... --content ...` |
| "Reply to that maintenance email" | `reply --id <id> --sender ... --content ...` |
| "Forward this to the plumber" | `forward --id <id> --sender ... --recipients ... --content ...` |
| "Search for emails about leaking" | `search --query "leaking"` |
| "Delete that spam email" | `delete --id <id> --folder INBOX` |
| "What emails have I sent?" | `list --folder SENT` |
| "How many unread emails?" | `mailboxes` (check unreadEmails count) |

## Notes

- Reading an email by ID or index automatically marks it as read
- Replies auto-populate the `Re:` subject prefix and send to the original sender
- Forwards auto-populate the `Fwd:` subject prefix
- The `sender` field should be the current user's email address

---
name: fintrack
description: Manage users, bank accounts, transactions, and subscriptions for a personal finance tracker. Use when the user asks about account balances, transaction history, spending analysis, or subscription management.
---

# FinTrack Personal Finance

Manage users, accounts, transactions, and subscriptions for personal finance tracking. Data is pre-loaded. No setup needed.

## Users

```bash
# List all users
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py users

# Get a specific user
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py user --id U001

# Search users by name or email
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py search-users --query "John"
```

## Accounts

```bash
# List all accounts
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py accounts

# Get a specific account
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py account --id A001

# Get all accounts for a user
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py accounts-by-user --user-id U001

# Search accounts by institution, type, or status
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py search-accounts --institution "Chase" --type "checking" --status "active"
```

### Account Filters

| Filter | Flag | Example |
|--------|------|---------|
| Institution | `--institution` | `--institution "Chase"` |
| Type | `--type` | `--type "checking"` |
| Status | `--status` | `--status "active"` |
| Limit | `--limit` | `--limit 20` |

## Transactions

```bash
# List all transactions
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py transactions --limit 50

# Get a specific transaction
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py transaction --id T001

# Get transactions for a user
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py transactions-by-user --user-id U001 --limit 20

# Search transactions by merchant, category, date, or amount
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py search-transactions --merchant "Amazon" --category "Shopping"
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py search-transactions --start "2026-03-01" --end "2026-03-15"
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py search-transactions --min-amount 100 --max-amount 500
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py search-transactions --user-id U001 --category "Groceries"

# Get spending breakdown by category for a user
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py spending-by-category --user-id U001
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py spending-by-category --user-id U001 --start "2026-02-01" --end "2026-02-28"
```

### Transaction Filters

| Filter | Flag | Example |
|--------|------|---------|
| Merchant | `--merchant` | `--merchant "Amazon"` |
| Category | `--category` | `--category "Groceries"` |
| Date range | `--start`, `--end` | `--start "2026-03-01" --end "2026-03-15"` |
| Amount range | `--min-amount`, `--max-amount` | `--min-amount 50 --max-amount 200` |
| User | `--user-id` | `--user-id U001` |
| Limit | `--limit` | `--limit 20` |

## Subscriptions

```bash
# List all subscriptions
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py subscriptions

# Get a specific subscription
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py subscription --id S001

# Get subscriptions for a user
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py subscriptions-by-user --user-id U001

# Get all active subscriptions
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py active-subscriptions

# Get upcoming billings within N days
python3 {baseDir}/fintrack_data.py upcoming-billings --days 7
```

## Answering Questions

| User asks | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| "Show me all users" | `users` |
| "What accounts does user U001 have?" | `accounts-by-user --user-id U001` |
| "Show my Chase accounts" | `search-accounts --institution "Chase"` |
| "What did I spend at Amazon?" | `search-transactions --merchant "Amazon"` |
| "How much did I spend on groceries?" | `search-transactions --category "Groceries"` |
| "Spending breakdown for March" | `spending-by-category --user-id U001 --start "2026-03-01" --end "2026-03-31"` |
| "Transactions over $500" | `search-transactions --min-amount 500` |
| "What subscriptions do I have?" | `subscriptions-by-user --user-id U001` |
| "What bills are coming up?" | `upcoming-billings --days 7` |
| "Show active subscriptions" | `active-subscriptions` |

---
name: messaging
description: Send and receive messages, manage conversations, and search message history. Use when the user asks about messages, conversations, chats, or wants to contact someone.
---

# Messaging

Send messages, browse conversations, and search message history. Data is pre-loaded. No setup needed.

## Viewing Conversations

```bash
# List all conversations (most recently updated first)
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py list

# List with pagination
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py list --offset 0 --limit 20

# Get a conversation with its messages
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py get --conversation-id CONV_ID

# Get with message pagination
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py get --conversation-id CONV_ID --offset 0 --limit 50

# Search conversations (searches title, participant names, message content)
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py search --query "invoice"

# Show raw data
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py show --offset 0 --limit 100
```

## Sending Messages

```bash
# Send a message to a user (creates a new conversation if none exists)
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py send --user-id USER_ID --content "Hello, how are you?"

# Send a message to an existing conversation
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py send-to --conversation-id CONV_ID \
    --content "Following up on the invoice."

# Send with an attachment
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py send --user-id USER_ID \
    --content "Please see the attached report." \
    --attachment "/path/to/report.pdf"
```

## Managing Conversations

```bash
# Create a new conversation with multiple participants
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py create \
    --participant-ids '["user_001", "user_002", "user_003"]' \
    --title "Q1 Budget Review"

# Create without a title (uses participant names)
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py create \
    --participant-ids '["user_001", "user_002"]'

# Add a user to an existing conversation
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py add-user \
    --conversation-id CONV_ID --user-id USER_ID
```

## Answering Questions

| User asks | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| "Show me my recent conversations" | `list` |
| "What did we talk about with John?" | `search --query "John"` |
| "Show me the conversation about invoices" | `search --query "invoice"` |
| "Send a message to user_042" | `send --user-id user_042 --content "..."` |
| "Reply to this conversation" | `send-to --conversation-id ... --content "..."` |
| "Start a group chat with Alice and Bob" | `create --participant-ids '["alice_id", "bob_id"]' --title "..."` |
| "Add Carol to this conversation" | `add-user --conversation-id ... --user-id carol_id` |

## Notes

- Conversations are sorted by `last_updated` descending — most recent first
- `send --user-id` will find an existing 1:1 conversation or create a new one
- Message timestamps are Unix timestamps
- `--participant-ids` and similar list arguments take JSON arrays

Applied styptic powder to one nail that bled slightly during trimming (normal).\n- Percy tolerated the procedure well (vocal but cooperative).\n\nRecommendations:\n- Monitor the trimmed nails over the next few days. Minor bleeding may occur but should stop quickly.\n- Provide a rough-textured perch (cement or pumice) to help maintain nail length naturally between visits.\n- Schedule a nail trim in 4-6 months if nails become overgrown again, or sooner if you notice similar darkening.\n- Continue Nekton-S supplement as previously prescribed.\n\nA PDF copy is attached.       "subject": "personal statement v2 - revision notes",
      "content": "v2 - better but still needs work\n\n---\n\nI have spent ten years as a technical recruiter, matching candidates with engineering roles at companies across the Pacific Northwest. The work has taught me to read people, understand organizational needs, and navigate complex stakeholder dynamics. It has also shown me, daily, how poorly designed the tools of my profession are.\n\nApplicant tracking systems reduce candidates to keyword matches. Interview platforms ignore accessibility for candidates across timezones. Algorithmic sourcing reinforces existing biases by surfacing the same profiles while overlooking nontraditional backgrounds. I have built workarounds for all of these problems, but workarounds are patches, not solutions.\n\nI am drawn to UW's HCDE program because it offers the research methods and design frameworks I need to move from identifying these problems to solving them. I want to learn how to conduct user research with both recruiters and job seekers, evaluate hiring technology through a human-centered lens, and prototype alternatives that serve a broader range of users.\n\nThe part-time format lets me stay grounded in my industry while building new skills. I plan to use coursework to investigate specific pain points I encounter daily, creating a feedback loop between professional experience and academic work.\n\nAfter completing the program I want to move into UX research or product design focused on HR technology, applying HCDE methods to the hiring systems that millions of people depend on.\n\n---\n\nnotes:\n- \"patches not solutions\" might be cliche, but it works. keep it?\n- need a specific example. the time I couldn't surface any women candidates for the devops role because linkedin kept showing the same 200 men. or the greenhouse screening thing where we auto-rejected 40 people because of a filter bug nobody noticed for two weeks.\n- \"rigorous user research\" was vague, I cut it. but should I name a specific method? contextual inquiry?\n- check word count limit again, this feels close\n- do I actually want to say \"move into UX research\"? that's basically announcing I want to leave recruiting. if someone at work somehow sees this... but it's a personal statement, not a linkedin post. keep it.\n- ask someone to read this? tamara would just ask questions about what HCDE is. maybe just sit with it one more week.",

      "parent_id": null,
      "attachments": {},
      "timestamp": "2026-04-13T08:00:00Z",
      "is_read": true

Minor bleeding may occur but should stop quickly.\n- Provide a rough-textured perch (cement or pumice) to help maintain nail length naturally between visits.\n- Schedule a nail trim in 4-6 months if nails become overgrown again, or sooner if you notice similar darkening.\n- Continue Nekton-S supplement as previously prescribed.\n\nA PDF copy is attached.       "subject": "personal statement v2 - revision notes",
      "content": "v2 - better but still needs work\n\n---\n\nI have spent ten years as a technical recruiter, matching candidates with engineering roles at companies across the Pacific Northwest. The work has taught me to read people, understand organizational needs, and navigate complex stakeholder dynamics. It has also shown me, daily, how poorly designed the tools of my profession are.\n\nApplicant tracking systems reduce candidates to keyword matches. Interview platforms ignore accessibility for candidates across timezones. Algorithmic sourcing reinforces existing biases by surfacing the same profiles while overlooking nontraditional backgrounds. I have built workarounds for all of these problems, but workarounds are patches, not solutions.\n\nI am drawn to UW's HCDE program because it offers the research methods and design frameworks I need to move from identifying these problems to solving them. I want to learn how to conduct user research with both recruiters and job seekers, evaluate hiring technology through a human-centered lens, and prototype alternatives that serve a broader range of users.\n\nThe part-time format lets me stay grounded in my industry while building new skills. I plan to use coursework to investigate specific pain points I encounter daily, creating a feedback loop between professional experience and academic work.\n\nAfter completing the program I want to move into UX research or product design focused on HR technology, applying HCDE methods to the hiring systems that millions of people depend on.\n\n---\n\nnotes:\n- \"patches not solutions\" might be cliche, but it works. keep it?\n- need a specific example. the time I couldn't surface any women candidates for the devops role because linkedin kept showing the same 200 men. or the greenhouse screening thing where we auto-rejected 40 people because of a filter bug nobody noticed for two weeks.\n- \"rigorous user research\" was vague, I cut it. but should I name a specific method? contextual inquiry?\n- check word count limit again, this feels close\n- do I actually want to say \"move into UX research\"? that's basically announcing I want to leave recruiting. if someone at work somehow sees this... but it's a personal statement, not a linkedin post. keep it.\n- ask someone to read this? tamara would just ask questions about what HCDE is. maybe just sit with it one more week.",

      "parent_id": null,
      "attachments": {},
      "timestamp": "2026-04-13T08:00:00Z",
      "is_read": true
    },

I will flag both in Thursday's deep-dive.\n\nAlso want to discuss the Datadog req. The hiring manager keeps revising the JD and I have lost two candidates because of it.\n\nOlivia",
      "cc": [],
      "parent_id": "e80162c5f7b9d46802ea25f7",
      "attachments": {},
      "timestamp": "2026-02-03T22:30:00Z",
      "is_read": true
    },
    {
      "email_id": "a94e17d3c82b065f49d1a6c3",
      "folder": "SENT",

      "recipients": [

      ],
      "subject": "Fwd: Visit Summary: Percy - January 13, 2026",
      "content": "Percy's vet report from Tuesday. He's all good! Dr. Vasquez wants him on a vitamin supplement now that he's over 5. Already ordering it.",
      "cc": [],
      "parent_id": null,
      "attachments": {
        "Percy_Visit_Summary_Jan2026.pdf": ""
      },
      "timestamp": "2026-01-17T05:20:00Z",
      "is_read": true
    },
    {
      "email_id": "817e3a2cf65d094b1a83c2e0",
      "folder": "SENT",
      "sender": "olivia.xie@gmail.com",
      "recipients": [
        "info@emeraldcityavian.com"
      ],
      "subject": "Re: Visit Summary: Percy - January 13, 2026",
      "content": "Thank you, this is great! Really glad he's healthy.\n\nI'm going to order the Nekton-S today. Just to confirm, the 1/4 teaspoon 3-4 times a week is right for his size? I want to make sure I don't overdo it.\n\nThanks again,\nOlivia",
      "cc": [],
      "parent_id": "53a8f6b1e04c27d983bf154e",
      "attachments": {},
      "timestamp": "2026-01-17T05:15:00Z",
      "is_read": true
    },
    {
      "email_id": "c5dd9cab8e9b6a84cb3afb9d",
      "folder": "SENT",
      "sender": "olivia.xie@gmail.com",
      "recipients": [
        "jessicalin.taipei@gmail.com"
      ],
      "subject": "Taiwan trip - hotel and itinerary recommendations",
      "content": "Hi Jessica,\n\nMy friend Tamara Brooks passed along your blog. Love the night market roundup. My wife and I are planning our first trip to Taiwan this June, about two weeks. Mostly Taipei but hoping to do a day trip to Jiufen and maybe a few days down south.\n\nWe're looking at Da'an district for our hotel. Any boutique recommendations that aren't too pricey? Hoping to stay around $120-150/night.\n\nAlso any restaurant recs for people who want great food without doing the whole Michelin circuit would be awesome.\n\nThanks!\nOlivia",
      "cc": [],
      "parent_id": null,
      "attachments": {},
      "timestamp": "2026-01-15T09:15:00Z",
      "is_read": true
    },
    {
      "email_id": "0a4f8c3e57b9d12605e3f7a8",
      "folder": "SENT",
      "sender": "olivia.xie@gmail.com",
      "recipients": [
        "jenna.reeves@gmail.com"
      ],
      "subject": "Fwd: Your first week with Nest: tips to save energy",
      "content": "worth looking at the eco temperature thing. if we set it to drop to 62 when were both at work we could save a decent amount on the electric bill",

      "attachments": {},

    },
    {

      ],
      "subject": "Re: Appointment Reminder: Percy - Tuesday, January 13",
      "content": "Thanks for the reminder! We'll be there Tuesday at 10:30.\n\nNo changes to report, he's been his usual self. Should I bring a sample of his current food or anything else?\n\nOlivia",

    "folder": "INBOX",
    "sender": "noreply@mychart.providence.org",
    "recipients": [
      "olivia.xie@gmail.com"
    ],
    "subject": "It's time to schedule your annual wellness visit",
    "content": "Hi Olivia,\n\nAccording to our records, you're due for your annual wellness exam with Dr. Sarah Kim.\n\nYour last visit: March 2025\n\nSchedule your appointment:\n- Online at mychart.providence.org\n- By phone: (206) 555-0637\n\nAnnual wellness visits are covered at 100% by most insurance plans.\n\nCapitol Hill Medical Group\n1600 E John St, Seattle, WA 98112\n\nManage your communication preferences at mychart.providence.org",
    "cc": [],
    "parent_id": null,
    "attachments": {},
    "timestamp": "2026-04-07T21:00:00Z",
    "is_read": false
  },
  {
    "email_id": "0a2e54e7b136789a124f0b9a",
    "folder": "INBOX",
    "sender": "carmen@delgadotax.com",
    "recipients": [
      "olivia.xie@gmail.com"
    ],
    "subject": "Re: Document Checklist for Your April 5 Appointment",
    "content": "Hi Olivia,\n\nGood question. Those monthly payments to Providence count as medical expenses for the year you paid them, so your 2025 payments are eligible.\n\nThe catch is that medical expenses are only deductible if they exceed 7.5% of your combined adjusted gross income, and only the amount over that threshold counts. For your income range you'd need over $11,000 in total medical expenses before it makes a difference.\n\nStill, bring the Providence statements. We'll add them up with any copays, prescriptions, dental, and vision expenses and see where you land. Sometimes it's closer than people think.\n\nCarmen",
    "cc": [],
    "parent_id": "d7fb21b4e8034567891cde67",
    "attachments": {},
    "timestamp": "2026-03-21T21:30:00Z",
    "is_read": true
  },
  {
    "email_id": "c6ea10a3d7923456780bcd56",
    "folder": "INBOX",
    "sender": "carmen@delgadotax.com",
    "recipients": [
      "olivia.xie@gmail.com"
    ],
    "subject": "Document Checklist for Your April 5 Appointment",
    "content": "Hi Olivia,\n\nHere's what to bring on the 5th. The more you have ready, the faster we'll get through it.\n\nFor both you and Jenna:\n- W-2s (Cascade Talent Partners for you, Mossback Creative for Jenna)\n- Any 1099 forms for freelance or side income\n- 1099-INT or 1099-DIV from bank or investment accounts (BECU, Fidelity, etc.)\n\nDeductions:\n- Medical expenses paid in 2025 (receipts, insurance statements, Providence Health payment records)\n- Charitable donations (receipts for anything over $250)\n- Your Fidelity 401(k) statement (contributions should also show on your W-2 but I like to cross-check)\n\nOther:\n- Copy of last year's return if you have notes on it (I have it on file here)\n- BECU routing and account number for direct deposit (I think we used your checking last year?)\n\nIf you're not sure about something just bring it and we'll figure it out.\n\nCarmen",
    "cc": [],
    "parent_id": null,
    "attachments": {},
    "timestamp": "2026-03-20T21:00:00Z",
    "is_read": true
  },
  {

    "folder": "INBOX",

      "olivia.xie@gmail.com"
    ],
    "subject": "Signed Lease Confirmation — Apt 3B, 1847 E Olive St",
    "content": "Hi Olivia,

Thank you for coming in to sign your renewal. Your new lease is now in effect.

Lease Summary:
Tenant(s): Olivia Xie, Jenna Reeves
Address: 1847 E Olive St, Apt 3B, Seattle, WA 98122
Term: June 1, 2026 – May 31, 2027
Monthly Rent: $2,275.00 (due on the 1st of each month)
Security Deposit: $2,200.00 (no change, carried over from prior lease)

A signed copy of your lease is attached for your records.

If you have any questions during your tenancy, don't hesitate to reach out.

Best,
Lisa Chen
Pacific Crest Property Management
1520 Broadway E, Suite 200
Seattle, WA 98102
(206) 555-0932
leasing@pacificcrestpm.com",

    "recipients": [
      "olivia.xie@gmail.com"
    ],
    "subject": "Appointment Confirmation: Percy - April 16, 2026",
    "content": "Thank you for scheduling Percy's appointment.

Appointment Confirmation
Patient: Percy (cockatiel)
Owner: Olivia Xie
Date: Thursday, April 16, 2026
Time: 10:00 AM
Type: Nail/Foot Concern
Provider: Dr. Linda Vasquez

Please arrive 5-10 minutes early. Bring Percy in a secure, covered carrier.

To reschedule or cancel, call (206) 555-0845 or reply to this email.

Emerald City Avian Clinic
4215 Stone Way N, Seattle, WA 98103
(206) 555-0845",
    "cc": [],
    "parent_id": null,
    "attachments": {},

      "olivia.xie@gmail.com"
    ],
    "subject": "Re: Lease Renewal Notice — Apt 3B, 1847 E Olive St",
    "content": "Hi Olivia,

Great, 12-month term it is. We have the following times available this week and next:

- Tuesday 4/14, 4:00 PM
- Wednesday 4/15, 5:30 PM
- Thursday 4/16, 10:00 AM

You and Jenna will both need to sign, so please choose a time that works for both of you. Bring a valid photo ID.

The signing takes about 15 minutes. Our office is at 1520 Broadway E, Suite 200 (buzzer #200).

See you soon!

Lisa Chen
Pacific Crest Property Management",
    "cc": [],
    "parent_id": "8f3e7d2b6a4c91f508d3b9e7",
    "attachments": {},
    "timestamp": "2026-04-11T01:00:00Z",
    "is_read": true
  },
  {

    "folder": "INBOX",
    "sender": "leasing@pacificcrestpm.com",
    "recipients": [
      "olivia.xie@gmail.com"
    ],
    "subject": "Re: Lease Renewal Notice — Apt 3B, 1847 E Olive St",
    "content": "Hi Olivia,

Thanks for getting back to us. Happy to answer your questions.

1. We offer 12-month and 18-month terms. The 18-month term locks in the $2,275 rate through November 2027. After that it would adjust to market rate.
2. We have roof work scheduled for mid-July, about two weeks. There may be some noise during business hours but it shouldn't affect your unit directly. We'll send a building notice before it starts.

Let us know which term you'd prefer and we can get the paperwork ready.

Best,
Lisa Chen
Pacific Crest Property Management
(206) 555-0932",

content.thinking:
Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me compile the full mid-year household brief. Let me organize all the data:

**Key facts to reconcile:**

1. **Taiwan Trip (now known to Jenna)**
   - EVA Air BR025: SEA→TPE Jun 15, 2026 depart 1:20 AM, arrive Jun 16 5:50 AM
   - EVA Air BR024: TPE→SEA Jun 25, 2026 depart 11:45 PM, arrive Jun 25 7:15 PM
   - Booking ref: TWXIE7K / XIETPE0615
   - Flight charged to card 6103 on Apr 8 for $1,882.40 — already paid
   - Hotel Proverbs Taipei booked for Jun 15-25 (10 nights, Deluxe Double), TWD 49,400 (~$1,512), also charged to card 6103 on Apr 8 — already paid
   - Free cancellation window closed as of today (Jun 16), so both bookings are locked in
   - Still need to complete online check-in and seat selection for the outbound flight
   - **URGENT**: The outbound flight BR025 actually departed yesterday morning — I should already be in Taipei by now

, having arrived this morning. The return flight is scheduled for June 25.

   - **HCDE Application — CRITICAL**: The June 1 deadline has passed. Today is June 16, and the application still shows missing components (personal statement and recommendation letters unchecked). There's no email confirmation of submission, so I need to determine if this was actually submitted or if the deadline was missed entirely.

3. **Lease Renewal**
   - New lease locked in for June 1, 2026 – May 31, 2027 at $2,275/month (up $75 from the previous rate)
   - Signed April 16 with Pacific Crest PM
   - First payment at the new rate was due June 1 — need to confirm it processed
   - Jenna's rent contribution will need adjustment from her current $1,100 share

4. **Financial Snapshot**
   - BECU Checking (3847): $3,900
   - BECU Savings (9215): $7,200
   - Chase Freedom Flex (6103): -$1,400
   - Fidelity account details cut off

5. **Ongoing Obligations**
   - 401(k) balance sitting at $112,000
   - Medical debt with Providence is down to around $2,850 after consistent $150 monthly payments through May, with the next payment due yesterday on June 15
   - Tax refund of $1,247 came through on April 24

6. **Jenna's Birthday Dinner**
   - Completed at Bateau on March 28 for a party of six at the Capitol Hill prix fixe rate of $85 per person

, plus $510 charged to card 6103 for the meal and a birthday candle, with attendees including Jenna, Tamara, Marcus, Nadia Khoury, and Sophie Aldrich

7. **Percy's Veterinary Care**
   - Annual wellness visit in January with Dr. Vasquez showed Percy's weight stable at 93g and started him on Nekton-S supplement for $135, then in April had overgrown nails trimmed for $45 with a pumice perch recommended, which I ordered from Amazon for $9.36 and arrived a couple days later, plus a Chewy order from January for $30.28 that was likely the supplement

8. **Upcoming 5K Race**
   - Registered for the Woodland Park 5K on May 17 for $38

9. **Race Day Logistics**
   - Tamara's picking me up at 8am

10. **Wine Country Trip**
    - Went to Woodinville for wine tasting on February 8 for Tamara's birthday with a shuttle from Pioneer Square, just the three of us with Jenna and Tamara

11. **Bike Maintenance**
    - Got a basic tune-up at Gregg's Cycles on March 25 for $85

12. **Tax Return**
    - Filed with Carmen Delgado on April 5 (technically April 6 due to timezone), paid $325, and received a $1,247 refund that deposited to my BECU checking account on April 24

13. **Smart Thermostat Installation**
    - Nest thermostat was installed, with an email from January mentioning the setup

, and the March report shows it's running well with 298 heating hours and an average temperature of 66.2°F; Olivia suggested setting it to 62 degrees when we're both away

14. **Annual Wellness Exam — OVERDUE**
    - Providence sent an email on April 7 saying I'm due for my annual wellness visit with Dr. Sarah Kim, and my last visit was in March 2025, but I haven't found any scheduling confirmation yet

15. **Dental Checkup**
    - Cascade Dental texted on May 1 about scheduling my 6-month checkup; my last cleaning was March 5 with Dr. Amy Chen, so the next one is due around September

16. **Active Subscriptions**
    - Looking at the next billing dates for all my current subscriptions, starting with iCloud+

, Strava, Spotify, Netflix, and Nintendo Switch Online

17. **HCDE Application — Still Pending**
    - I've had multiple writing sessions since February, sent myself a personal statement draft on April 13, and reviewed it against the rubric on May 22, but the May 19 email reminder shows the personal statement and recommendation letters are still incomplete, and I haven't submitted anything yet with the June 1 deadline approaching

18. **PTO Calculation for Taiwan Trip**
    - Starting with 12.5 days accrued as of January 31, plus 1.25 days accruing each month, I'll have around 18.1 days available by mid-June
    - The Taiwan trip from June 15-25 will need about 8 work days of PTO since I'm leaving Monday morning and returning Thursday evening

, covering Jun 15-20 and Jun 23-25
    - This should have been submitted at least two weeks in advance per company policy

17. **Rent increase effective June 1**
    - New rate is $2,275, up from $2,200
    - Need to verify if June 1 payment went through at the new rate
    - Should discuss with Jenna about adjusting her $1,100 contribution

18. **Denise's check-in email from May 21 — still unread**
    - She mentioned noticing I've seemed off and was late to standup a couple times
    - I need to respond to this

19. **EVA Air seat selection**
    - Email from May 20 about BR025, but if I'm already in Taipei that's irrelevant for the outbound flight
    - The return flight BR024 on Jun 25 might still need seat selection

20. **Providence payment due yesterday**
    - June 15 deadline has passed
    - Haven't seen a June transaction in the ledger yet

, only entries through May 30
    - Need to confirm if it was actually paid

Now I'm tracking the medical debt payments over time. Looking at the Providence emails, I can see the payment history: started at $3,600 in January, then $150 monthly payments brought it down to $2,850 by May. The June 15 payment status is still unclear from what I can see.

For savings, the emergency fund is at $7,200 toward a $15,000 target, while the Taiwan trip fund got mostly depleted with flights and hotel totaling about $3,400. I've been adding roughly $1,350 net to savings across the first five months of the year through regular transfers.

I should double-check if there are any June transactions showing the new rent rate and the June payment to Providence, but the transaction data I have only goes through May 30.
